properties.remark: Emergency Management reported a closed road due to flash flooding in Palm Valley near the intersection of Queens Sago Dr and Stuart Place Rd.
Emergency Manager reported water being pumped out a house due to flash flooding.
Emergency Manager reported a stranded vehicle on the road due to flash flooding.
Water of unknown depth into the elementary school. Heavy rain was continuing at the time. Start time of flooding unknown. Reported by Texas Dept. of Transportation.
Public reported water getting into a building.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from Penitas, TX. Based on radar evidence, there was strong evidence of a tornadic circulation. Emergency manager reported trees downs and power outages.
Emergency manager reported trees downs and power outages.
2 to 3 feet of water flooding FM 755 in northeast Starr County near Santa Elena. Actual start time unknown.

Corrects previous tornado report from Edcouch. A NWS Damage Survey was done on 03/28/2025. It was determined that a EF-0 tornado briefly touched down about 1.5 miles east of Edcouch at 3:50 PM CDT. Please refer to the Public Information Statement for more details on the tornado event.
1 to 2 feet of water at 15th and Business 83, some into businesses.
location Frontage at 29th Street (McAllen Conv. Center Road). Several vehicles with water above hoods...3 to 4 feet of water depth.
Webcam still showed estimated 2 to 3 feet of water depth at intersection of Cage and Ferguson. Time estimated; flooding may have started earlier.
Several homes with unknown water depth inside. Time estimated.
Brief touchdown, at TX Highway 107 and 2 Mile Rd. Some damage to structures.
More than two dozen roads closed city-wide and up to forty homes with unknown water depth inside. Time was the report time from a press release and flooding likely began closer to 4 PM.

Unknown number of homes with some water incursion in west Harlingen and frontage road closed due to high water at Stuart Place. Valley international airport recorded more than 5 inches in about 90 minutes.
Swift water rescues underway in the city. Water depth unknown but rainfall rates of 2.5 inches per hour for two hours had likely occurred.
Water rescues were underway in the City of Mercedes, according to the EMC of the County and City. Water depth unknown. Report time is for the rescues but not the start of the flooding.
EM of Hidalgo County relayed report of water rescues in Sebastian (Willacy County).
Social media report of 1.5 inch in Fronton TX. Time estimated by radar.
Zapata County Sheriffs office stated public reported hail generally the size of golf balls with some hail the size of softballs in the city of San Ygnacio.
Broadcast media shared video from resident with estimated quarter-sized hail at the intersection of Mile 9 and 1015.
Broadcast media shared resident report of estimated 1 inch hail in Weslaco. Time estimated based on radar data.
Report of estimated baseball hail size received by trained storm spotter at the intersection of FM2687 and FM649.
500 acres of corn destroyed.
Ranch owner sent video of estimated quarter sized hail.
Report from mPING: Golf Ball (1.75 in.).
Pictures and video on X showed ping pong to golfball size hail north of Rio Grande City.
Member of the public posted Facebook video of downed fence and trees.
Report received from HAM operator of 1 inch estimated hail falling for 10 minutes duration.
Member of the public sent photos through Facebook message showing approximately golf ball-sized hail.
Local EM reported estimated ping pong ball sized hail at residence and provided a photo.
Member of the public posted Facebook photo comment showing approximately golf ball sized hailstone.
Member of the public posted a photo comment via Facebook showing approximately 1 inch hail.
Photo received from DHR Health of a 2 inch sized measured hailstone that fell near Dove and McColl.
Member of the public sent a photo through a Facebook message showing hailstones approximately 2 inches.
Member of the public left a Facebook comment with video showing approximately golf ball sized hail at this intersection. Reported minor vehicle damage, as well as damage to vegetation.
Member of the public posted Facebook photo comment with location and timestamp, approximately 1 inch.
Report from mPING: Half Dollar (1.25 in.).
Trained Skywarn spotter reported Golf ball sized hail around the intersection of Wisconsin and Veterans.
Member of the public posted a Facebook photo comment showing an approximately golf ball sized hailstone taken at this location.
Member of the public pasted a Facebook comment of a photo of hailstone approximately 3 inches long and 1.5 inches wide.
Member of the public posted Facebook photo (location verified by radar) of a hailstone estimated about 1.75 inches.
Trained spotted reported receiving estimated golf ball sized hail on vehicle driving on the expressway just NE of Donna.
Member of the public posted Facebook photo verified location based on radar of a hailstone approximately 1.5 inches.
Member of the public provided Facebook photo comment showing approximately 2 inch hail.
Public report of small and large trees down.
A NWS Employee reported metal roofing structures and a fence blown over near the intersection of Benito Ramirez and Terry Road in Edinburg, TX.
Hidalgo County Fire Marshal reported a power pole leaning along FM 490 just west of US 281/IH-69C. Time and duration estimated by radar.
Hidalgo County Fire Marshal reported at least two large trees blown down, including into onto the road, along FM 2825 near the intersections of Sunshine and Val Verde Road. Time is radar estimated.
Microburst from a severe thunderstorm caused the roof of a single-family residential home to be ripped off on Roman Circle.
Microburst from a severe thunderstorm damaged a single-family residential home on Roman Circle and caused its roof to be ripped off.
Microburst from a severe thunderstorm resulted in a partial collapse of a single-family home that was under construction on El Dora Rd near N Tower Rd.
Microburst from a severe thunderstorm caused a metal standalone tower at a local high school to be bent perpendicular to its base.

Microburst from a severe thunderstorm caused 15 power poles along El Dora Rd near N Val Verde Rd to fall to the ground. A small building also partially collapsed along El Dora Rd, which was confirmed by Emergency Management drone footage.
Correction: A NWS Employee reported metal roofing structures and a fence blown over near the intersection of Benito Ramirez and Terry Road in Edinburg, TX. Time radar estimated.
Microburst from a severe thunderstorm caused the traffic lights pole to collapse at the intersection of Sioux Rd and N Val Verde Rd.
Microburst from a severe thunderstorm flipped a trailer onto a road next to a local high school, damaging a fence in the process.
AWOS station KTXW Weslaco Midvally.
Video (via Facebook) showing numerous homes and structures damaged. Stop sign blown over.
Report from mPING: Street/road flooding; Street/road closed; Vehicles stranded.
Weslaco Emergency Management reported a residence trailer was rolled during strong winds just north of the expressway near Mile 10 and Mile 4 1/2 West with no injuries. Time estimated from radar.
**Corrects previous report from South Padre Island to change from wind to landspout/waterspout.**
Law enforcement reports golfball sized hail in Sarita, TX. Time estimated from radar.
Border Patrol reports golfball sized hail at a checkpoint 14 miles south of Sarita, TX, 6.5 miles north of Armstrong, TX.
Public posted social media video showing at least golf ball size hail at the Sarita Rest Stop along US-77.
Public reported hail of 1.00 inch 1 mile east of Los Fresnos, TX.
A retired NWS Employee reported a tree and a large tree branch down.
Public reported estimated hail size of 1.25 inches in Arroyo City, TX.
NWS Brownsville observed and measured hail of 1.25 inches at the weather forecast office just northeast of the Brownsville South Padre International Airport.

Corrects previous hail report from 2 E Brownsville to 4 E. NWS Brownsville observed and measured hail of 1.25 inches at the weather forecast office just northeast of the Brownsville South Padre International Airport.
Public reported quarter sized hail 5 miles east of Brownsville, TX.
